| :---- |

# ---

# Product Strategy for Change.org Core Experience \- 2026

Change.org is building the modern operating system for democracy \- identifying the public will and mobilizing hundreds of millions of people to act on it. Petitions are a critical part of helping people take action, but today the **experience starts over with every campaign** \[*1x data*\] .

The product story we want to be able to tell in 2027: The hundreds of millions of people who have started or signed petitions at Change.org will have **a civic home that builds around them** \- one that knows their cause history, always has something relevant to act on, and makes their history visible and worth building together. 

As social platforms optimize for outrage, and civic tools treat participation as a transaction, Change.org will stand alone as the place where **participation is personal**, and every action builds toward something bigger than the campaign that started it.

---

## Key Insights 

**👉Change.org has campaigning data and expertise that no generic tool has.** LLMs give advice, but Change.org can give evidence of what has worked in this category, at this scale, in this location, combining platform insights with in-house expertise. By June 2027 (12mo): a Starter CoPilot will be trained on this data and pairing with Starters throughout their campaigns.

**👉Today, the experience resets with each petition.** Petitions look the same on Day 1 as Day 100, return visits are almost entirely email-driven \[*data*\], and there's almost no memory of who you are or what you've done. We (ask Starters to) re-acquire supporters every time. By end of period: Supporter history lives in the Civic Home, their record grows with every action, and leaving means losing something real.

**👉Personalization has an input AND an output challenge.** One signature isn’t enough to know someone, and we haven’t designed other ways for supporters to engage. Matching against 500k petitions is solvable, but a fresh, high-quality supply of petitions is the real constraint for an engaging Civic Home. By end of period: a new identity experience captures who you are in your first days and moments post-sign, and AI-generated petition stubs from news and social media expand petition supply infinitely, solving both sides.

**👉The post-sign moment is an emotional highpoint, and we’re not using it to build a relationship.** The platform has not initiated a relationship with supporters outside of a petition. The post-sign moment is the highest-leverage point we have, and with onboarding tests in Q2 we are asking the PSF to do the work of an entire relationship or product journey because we have nowhere else to do it. By end of period: post-sign has a clear ask and the Civic Home has a relationship to build on.

## Product Vision & Key Bets

| ![][image1] |
| :---: |

If Change.org moves from a petition platform to an ongoing Civic Home for starters and supporters, we need to do an incredible job at:

1. Giving Starters a **built-in, engaged audience** matched to their cause, and a Co-Pilot built on Change.org i**ntelligence that knows campaigning better** than any general LLM. This makes it more likely that their petition will be successful, and gives them incentive to campaign again at [Change.org](http://Change.org).

2. Giving Supporters a reason to return to Change.org unprompted. They will see petitions they actually care about, **always have something relevant** to do or contribute to, and their **cause history and personalized profile** will make the platform difficult to leave.

The primary metric for success is Supporter Retention, which would describe an experience that has become powerful enough for both Starters and Supporters to return to and build together.

These are the leading indicators for understanding whether we are on the path towards this kind of experience:

| Metric | Now | Target | Why |
| :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| Supporter NPS | baseline | \+xx | Are Supporters satisfied with the experience? |
| Supporter Week 2 return rate (non-triggered) | baseline | \+xx | Is Civic Home creating unprompted engagement? |
| W2 Starter Retention | baseline | \+xx | Do Starters stay engaged with campaigns? |
| CoPilot Petition Success | – | predictor | Is CoPilot the right bet? |

### Starters \- From Solo Petition-Maker to Confident Starter

The bet for Starters in 2026 is an AI Co-Pilot trained on Change.org’s own petition data and expertise, that supports them throughout their first petition and beyond. This builds on the AI-Guidance Beta in Q1 and helps Starters activate Supporters to contribute updates, endorsements, and shares, so that the petition stays alive without the Starter carrying it alone.

Every petition that runs through Change.org makes this expertise more precise and more actionable for starters, and we can present this expertise in moments where we know they are experiencing highs (ie *you have 400 signatures \- petitions like yours that broke into local press did so around 500\. Here’s a draft note to ask your supporters to help*), or lows (*your signatures have slowed down, but that’s normal at this stage. A personal video update can help \- do you want to work together to post one?*). This is the difference between generic LLM guidance and evidence, and no external tool can replicate it.

The Co-Pilot also becomes the mechanic for starter retention over time \- it not only knows the starter’s why, the petition’s journey, and what to do next, but it also becomes more useful the deeper into a campaign they go. For the starters who go on to run multiple campaigns, the Co-Pilot knows their history and compounds across petitions. Leaving Change.org means losing a co-pilot built specifically around their campaigns and their values.   

| ![][image2] |
| :---: |

A starter equipped with proprietary campaign intelligence produces stronger, more credible petitions, another input to public will (beyond signature counts).

### Supporters \- From Signers to Regulars

The bet for Supporters in 2026 is a persistent home \- a surface that captures who you are as an active participant in democracy, serves personalized petitions from a continuously expanding supply, and gives you an active role in campaigns beyond the initial signature.

Leave Change.org and lose the record of the things you stood for \- causes backed, campaigns contributed to, a history built over years. New content makes this home worth returning to over and over again \- AI-generated “stubs” from news and social media expand the universe of petitions so there is always something relevant to act on. The more a Supporter engages, the more precisely this experience reflects who they are, and when the CoPilot routes specific asks to them they feel needed rather than just notified.

This bet requires Supporters to share more about who they are and what they care about, and if we can’t drive engagement (due to crowded PSF, lack of trust, or otherwise) we may consider status, visible impact, or community belonging as the stronger retention strategy.

### The Engagement Loop Overall

**On a specific petition**  
Starters create petitions and the CoPilot helps Starters activate Supporters. Supporters signal what they care about in the Civic Home/PSF, and Starters gain a built in audience already matched to their cause. Petition updates are the connective tissue: the mechanism that makes the Starter-Supporter relationship active rather than transactional, and the signal that tells Change.org what to surface next.

**Across petitions**

## In 2026

#### Top Hypotheses

We’ll increase the \# or frequency of supporters returning to **a specific petition** if …

* …the starter sends a meaningful update tied to a real milestone  
* …we make the supporter’s contribution visible when something changes  
* …we surface opportunities to help when the supporter is (emotionally) invested & ready  
  * …by helping starters reach out at the right moments  
  * …by adding new ways to engage between signatures (jobs, follows, starts)

We’ll increase the \# or frequency of supporters returning **to Change.org** if …

* …we help them build value here that they would miss if they left  
  * …by knowing and reflecting their values (not just activity)  
  * …by giving petition activity a home  
  * …by creating new ways to view collective action across issues  
* …we increase the inventory of campaigns or activities to match them with  
  * …by using news or social media to seed new, potential petitions

We’ll increase the confidence of starters if …

* …we make campaign guidance feel relevant and personal  
  * …by leveraging what we know about them to coach or advise  
* …we proactively supporting them throughout the campaign  
* …we give them access to engaged supporters on the platform  
* …we share the expertise of all of the petitions that came before theirs

#### High-Level Roadmap

*\*\*example only: exploring dependencies and sequencing\*\**

| Focus | Q1 | Q2 | Q3 | Q4 |
| :---- | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| **User Milestone** |  |  |  |  |
| **Credibility System** Supporter Exp | EndorsementsMomentum Bar Design Sprint | Petition Updates AI Summaries | Petition Stages | TBD |
| **Identity Signals** Personalization | Onboarding Experiments | Onboarding cont Connect signal to recommendations | Identity on Profile | Following & Start |
| **Starter CoPilot**Starter Exp \+ Apps | AI Guidance Checklist | Auto-Drafted Updates; Campaign Stages | Copilot x Petition Data | Activate Supporters |
| **Civic Home**PersonalizationSupporter Exp | TBD | SPH FoundationConcept Testing | HistoryIdentity | Personalized FeedFollowing & Start |
| **Infinite Petitions**PersonalizationSite Platform | TBD | News \+ Social Data Pipeline Spike | Petition “Stubs” from news | Personalized Feed |

## 

## What We’re Not Doing

**A Social-Style Community Feed as the primary Supporter Experience.** Meaningful action is Change.org’s advantage, not content consumption, and we will consistently lose to social media platforms if we try to compete on ugc/feed mechanics. Consider revisiting this decision if the bet on identity/home is not working, or if content is the primary driver of return visits. 

**Starting with a Supporter App.** A new surface with nothing to run on adds complexity before we are ready. Revisit timing once Supporter Retention hits a threshold of xx.

**Direct integration with Elections or Deliberations.** The core experience needs to build the identity and engagement infrastructure that supports these pillars, but prematurely combining the products is not the priority in this time horizon.

**Global Testing.** The identity/home bet and the copilot need to prove out in one market before scaling to a different democratic context. We can revisit this decision if non-US growth outpaces US, if we decide that there is a stronger test market outside of the US, or if we feel the applicability to multiple democracies must be proven first.

### 

### Strategic Screen

Other viable strategic focus areas, ranked by:

| Strategy | Democracy OS; Supports Elections & Deliberations | Essential to Powerful Petition Platform | Differentiated / Leverages [C.org](http://C.org) strengths | Retention Fit | Right Time; [C.org](http://C.org) readiness | User \<3 or Demand |
| :---- | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| History (144) | 3 \- high | 2 \- med | 3 \- high | 2 \- med | 2 \- med | 2 \- med |
| Starter-Supporter Community Experience (54) | 3 \- high | 2 \- med | 1 \- low | 3 \- high | 1 \- low | 3 \- high |
| Signed Petition Home for Retention (16) | 1 \- low | 2 \- med | 2 \- med | 1 \- low | 2 \- med | 2 \- med |
| Supporter App with Feed-like content for Retention (6) | 2 \- med | 1 \- low | 1 \- low | 3 \- high | 1 \- low | 1 \- low |
| Status and Gamification for Retention (4) | 1 \- low | 1 \- low | 1 \- low | 2 \- med | 1 \- low | 2 \- med |

### 

### Risks & Open Questions

**Credibility System doesn’t materialize.**  **Risk: high// Likelihood: Medium**  
Credibility is infrastructure for an identity product, the risk is that we approach this as petition-page experiments or cleanup rather than a product investment.

**Identity vs Collaboration.** **Risk: high// Likelihood: Medium**  
The Supporter experience bet pulls in two directions \- a personal civic home (my cause history, my identity as a civic actor) and active contribution / collaboration (help keep campaigns alive, reduce starter burden). These aren’t in conflict but prioritize differently in the roadmap, and the risk is that we do a little bit of each without seeing Supporter returns materialize.

**AI erodes trust.**  **Risk: high// Likelihood: Medium**  
The ME PAGE, infinite petition supply, and the Co-pilot are all heavily AI-driven; if users perceive the platform as manipulating public will rather than reflecting it, trust will be undermined. Need to build transparency into AI-generated content (sourcing, framing, how recommendations work).

**CoPilot Scope Creep.** **Risk: medium// Likelihood: High**  
If the Starter CoPilot scope expands to cover every AI nudge, it will lose its sharpness as a product surface of its own. Needs early concept testing and clear use-cases to keep it focused (and radically move anything else out into its own feature).

**Revenue vs Identity Investment.**  **Risk: high// Likelihood: Low**  
The revenue model (promote, membership) is proven. Identity requires investment in surfaces without direct revenue proxies \- at what point does this need to shift?

### Cross-Pillar Notes

**Core x Elections:** Without Civic Identity, Elections has no way to find people by cause affinity or actions, which is a distribution advantage Change.vote has over a generic voter guide.

**Core x Deliberations:** When the Civic Home is working, Deliberations can distribute outputs to a cause-matched, engaged audience. The challenge becomes activation on deliberation-triggered petitions instead of cold outreach.

Key Changes

* To know your cause history → Give petition activity a home  
* To proactively surface opportunities → Increase inventory of campaigns or activities to match you with  
* To proactively surface opportunities → Create new ways to understand you and your values  
* To proactively surface opportunities → Create new opportunities to participate between signatures  
* To make collective impact visible → Create new ways to view collective action across issues
